office_does: One of two state representatives for the 32nd Legislative District (Shoreline, Lynnwood, Mountlake Terrace, Edmonds area), serving a two-year term voting on state law and budgets.
race_blurb: An intra-Democratic contest: incumbent Lauren Davis, known for her behavioral-health legislative record, is challenged by Imraan Siddiqi, Executive Director of CAIR-Washington, who runs with a progressive-movement coalition. Both advance to the November general election.

candidates.summary: Incumbent Democratic state representative since 2019 whose career centers on behavioral health, including founding the Washington Recovery Alliance and championing Ricky's Law.
highlights: Passed Ricky's Law (involuntary crisis commitment for life-threatening addiction) and created the Recovery Navigator Program, plus treatment programs inside jails and ERs [S1][S2][S3]
Has "championed healthcare for immigrants and protections against ICE" and describes herself as "completely pro-choice" [S1]
Endorsed by Governors Ferguson and Gregoire, Representatives DelBene, Larsen, and Smith, the State Labor Council, and a broad union slate [S1]

experience.basis: Seven-year incumbent with one of the Legislature's most identifiable single-issue records (Ricky's Law, Recovery Navigator Program).

safety.basis: Treatment-and-recovery-led record (Recovery Navigator, jail/ER treatment, crisis care center), tempered by the involuntary-commitment mechanism of Ricky's Law.

immigration.basis: States she has championed healthcare for immigrants and protections against ICE.

spending.basis: Record is built on funding and expanding programs: treatment access, domestic-violence victim services, and the Lynnwood Crisis Care Center.

candidates.summary: First-time Democratic candidate and Executive Director of CAIR-Washington, a civil rights advocate and former small business owner challenging the incumbent with a progressive-movement coalition.
highlights: Core planks are protecting immigrant communities facing "federal targeting" and building "a Washington that stands up to federal overreach" [S1][S2]
One of the original plaintiffs against the Trump administration's "Muslim Ban"; organizes coalitions with labor unions and tenant-rights advocates [S1]
Reported endorsements include U.S. Reps. Rashida Tlaib and Ilhan Omar, 32nd LD seatmate Rep. Cindy Ryu, UFCW 3000 (dual), and the Transit Riders Union [S3]

experience.basis: First-time candidate with no elected record, explicitly framing the race as current leaders not doing enough.

immigration.basis: CAIR-Washington executive director, Muslim Ban plaintiff, and a platform centered on protecting immigrants from federal targeting.

housing.basis: Pledges to fight for housing affordability and organizes coalitions with tenant-rights advocates.
